Cortex Code Video Script: Agent Teams
column11: Let Agent Teams Do the Work
column12: Delegate Complex Projects to an Agent Team | Cortex Code CLI
column13:
When data projects get complex, a single agent isn't enough, and coordinating the rest still makes you a bottleneck. 

Cortex Code CLI changes that. It is a data-native AI coding agent with Snowflake expertise built in that delegates complex projects to a team of specialized agents. Each agent starts with full context, a shared task list keeps everyone aligned, and the team shuts down cleanly when the work is done.

Watch to learn:

📋 Parallel team-based execution: How Cortex Code CLI proactively forms a team, decomposes work into a shared task list, assigns specialists, and coordinates completion across multiple phases, all without you managing the handoffs

🔗 Context Continuity: How named output variables carry results forward through the pipeline so agents build on each other's work without redundant re-prompting

🚦 Human-in-the-Loop: Use Ctrl+S to manage, approve, and unblock your agent team at any point in the process

👉 Get started with Cortex Code CLI: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/cortex-code/cortex-code-cli

Cortex Code Video Script: Cortex AI Functions
column12: From Raw PDFs to a Queryable Table in One Prompt | Cortex Code
column13:
Extracting value from raw, unstructured data like PDFs, images, and audio shouldn't require complex OCR and custom parsers. In this video, see how Snowflake Cortex AI turns SQL into a multimodal query language and how Cortex Code CLI automates the entire process for you.

What you'll learn:

📄 Document Intelligence: Parse raw invoices from multiple vendors into a structured, queryable Snowflake table from a single prompt


🖼️ Multimodal Analytics: Analyze text, images, and audio natively alongside structured data with no data movement required


🤖 Agentic Coding: Describe what you want in plain language and let Cortex Code CLI handle the extraction logic and syntax

Links & Resources:
🚀 Get started with Cortex Code CLI: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/cortex-code/cortex-code-cli
🛠️ Try Cortex AI Functions: https://snowflake.com/en/developers/guides/getting-started-with-cortex-aisql/
📖 Full Documentation: https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/snowflake-cortex/aisql

column12: Build In Snowflake Faster With Cortex Code CLI
column13: Learn how Cortex Code CLI brings Snowflake expertise into your terminal or favorite IDE, so you can turn plain-language prompts into real work inside Snowflake. Explore built-in skills for pipelines, semantic views, and agents, plus how to extend Cortex Code with your own skills.

column1: dbt Development in a Single Prompt
column8: Q1 Coco + DE Use Cases Video Briefs
column11: dbt Development in a Single Prompt
column12: Build dbt Projects in Snowflake with a Single Prompt
column13: Build a complete dbt project in Snowflake with a single prompt using Cortex Code CLI. See how Cortex Code scans source tables, generates models, adds tests, runs builds, validates outputs, and produces a shareable report, all from your terminal. What used to take hours of context-switching across tools now takes minutes.

column1: Optimizing dbt for speed and cost
column8: Q1 Coco + DE Use Cases Video Briefs
column12: Optimize dbt Projects for Speed With Cortex Code
column13: dbt projects are easy to build, but keeping them fast and cost-efficient is another challenge. As source tables grow and models become more complex, runtimes increase and performance issues get harder to pinpoint. In this demo, Cortex Code CLI analyzes your project’s run_results.json, identifies the slowest models, flags unused ones, and suggests specific optimizations you can apply immediately, all from a single prompt in your terminal.

column1: Airflow - Debugging a failed DAG run
column8: Q1 Coco + DE Use Cases Video Briefs
column12: Debug Apache Airflow® DAG Failures With Snowflake Cortex Code
column13: Learn how Cortex Code CLI helps you debug a failed Airflow DAG run end to end, from task status to dbt logs and Snowflake validation. See how a single prompt traces a failed dbt model, inspects the model SQL, checks upstream dependencies in Snowflake, and identifies the root cause, a broken source table. This workflow fits Airflow running locally, on Kubernetes, or on MWAA, and supports follow-up prompts for data quality checks and downstream impact analysis.

column8: Q1 Coco + DE Use Cases Video Briefs
column12: Make Your dbt Core Builds Faster with Cortex Code
column13: When dbt builds fail or pipelines slow down, Cortex Code CLI accelerates troubleshooting by bringing Snowflake expertise directly to your terminal. By reading your project files—including models, tests, and lineage—without requiring a manual compile, it can instantly identify bottlenecks, perform blast radius analysis, and audit test coverage.  This data-native AI agent combines dbt project structure with Snowflake query history to provide a prioritized improvement plan, allowing you to trace dependencies and refactor code from a single prompt with total confidence.

column8: Q1 Coco Use Cases Video Briefs
column12: Rapid Data Exploration with Cortex Code CLI
column13: Data exploration usually means inspecting schemas, profiling columns, and writing exploratory code just to understand what’s there. In this demo, we use Cortex Code CLI to handle that work directly from the terminal on real Snowflake data. Anything you want to understand in your data, just ask Cortex Code.

column12: Ship AI Agents Fast with Cortex Code CLI
column13: Vibe-coded agents can look impressive, but they often become brittle when exposed to real enterprise data. In this walkthrough, we use Cortex Code CLI to build a Cortex Agent powered directly by governed Snowflake data. We combine structured metrics, unstructured search, routing logic, and guardrails, then deploy to Snowflake Intelligence.
